name: sca_modulecheck_full_recovery
description: SCA integrity check storm with automatic full recovery — after receiving ChecksumMismatch, each agent immediately launches a ModuleFull session (207 DataValues). Models the real recovery cycle: mismatch detected -> agent re-syncs everything. Combines M2 (IndexerQueue blocking) + M1 (WorkersQueue burst) + M3 (RocksDB growth).
notes: CRITICAL SCENARIO: The most dangerous SCA recovery cycle.
Combines all three top hotspots: M2 (IndexerQueue) + M1 (WorkersQueue) + M3 (RocksDB).
Chain of events per agent:
1. Agent sends Start(mode=ModuleCheck) + ChecksumModule(wrong hash) + End.
2. Manager blocks up to 50s in IndexerQueue calculating checksum.
3. Manager responds EndAck(ChecksumMismatch).
4. Agent immediately launches ModuleFull: Start(mode=0) + 207 DataValues + End.
5. 207 DataValues enter WorkersQueue (M1) -> workers write to RocksDB (M3).
6. Completed session enters IndexerQueue (M2) -> worker reads RocksDB + bulkIndex.
Memory accumulation (30 agents):
- WorkersQueue: 30 x 207 x ~5KB = ~31MB peak if enqueue > dequeue rate.
- RocksDB: 30 x 207 x 5KB = ~30MB in queue/inventory_sync/.
- IndexerQueue: 30 Response{Context} objects with full session metadata.
SCA-specific dimension: this is what happens in production after an indexer maintenance
window. All agents detect mismatch on the first ModuleCheck after recovery and
simultaneously trigger ModuleFull — double-queue saturation + disk spike.
data_size=207 matches the real CIS Ubuntu 22.04 policy (207 checks, ~5KB each).
Real check payload captured from Ubuntu 5x agent (see sample_payloads/sca_check_template.json).
Expected proxies:
- RSS: ramp during ModuleCheck phase (IndexerQueue backlog), then spike during ModuleFull.
- rocksdb_dir_bytes: grows to 30 x 207 x 5KB = ~30MB during ModuleFull phase.
- indexer_q: should show backlog in manager logs after mismatch confirmed.
- end_ack_error: counts ChecksumMismatch responses (counted as error in sender).
Compare with sca_modulecheck_storm: same ModuleCheck phase, but adds the ModuleFull
recovery flood that is the actual OOM trigger in production.
